fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Rhode Island Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as an occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Rhode Island - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity rhode island, how to transfer occupational therapist license to rhode island, occupational therapist compact rhode island, rhode island occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: Rhode Island DOH — OT Licensure
board.mailing_address: Rhode Island Department of Health, Health Professions Regulation, 3 Capitol Hill, Room 105, Providence, RI 02908-5097

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) exam

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in Rhode Island.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Access the Online System
steps.description: Visit the RI Department of Health Online Licensing System, click 'Apply for Initial Licensure/Certification', select 'Occupational Therapist', and create a new user account if you do not have one.

steps.title: Complete Application & Pay Fee
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the online application form and pay the $100 initial licensure fee via the RI Department of Health Online Licensing System.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for your official academic transcripts, verifying your degree from an accredited occupational therapy program, to be sent directly from your institution to the RI Department of Health.

steps.title: Verify NBCOT Certification
steps.description: Ensure that the National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) sends verification of your successful examination directly to the Rhode Island Department of Health.

steps.title: Undergo Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Complete the required state and federal criminal background check as per instructions provided by the RI Department of Health after application submission.

steps.title: Provide Supporting Documents
steps.description: Submit any additional required documentation, such as proof of name change or licensure verification from other states, as requested by the Department of Health.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: Rhode Island offers a temporary occupational therapy permit for new graduates who have completed an ACOTE-accredited program and are awaiting their NBCOT exam results. The temporary permit allows practice under the direct supervision of a licensed OTR. Contact the Rhode Island DOH — OT Licensure for current temporary permit requirements and duration limits.
reciprocity_text: Occupational therapists licensed in another state may apply for licensure in Rhode Island through endorsement. Submit a completed application to the Rhode Island DOH — OT Licensure, along with official license verification from your current state and proof of active NBCOT certification. Processing typically takes 4 to 8 Weeks.

faqs.question: How much does a Rhode Island OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The total initial application and licensing fee is approximately $110. Check the board website for the latest fee schedule.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does Rhode Island have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers occupational therapist practice in Rhode Island. To work in Rhode Island, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to Rhode Island?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Rhode Island while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Occupational therapists licensed in another state may apply for licensure in Rhode Island through endorsement. Submit a completed application to the Rhode Island DOH — OT Licensure, along with official license verification from your current state and proof of active NBCOT certification. Processing typically takes 4 to 8 Weeks.

official_links.portal_name: RI Department of Health Online Licensing System
official_links.registration_steps: Visit the RI Department of Health Online Licensing System and click 'Apply for Initial Licensure/Certification'. Select 'Occupational Therapist', then create a new user account with your personal details and desired username/password. Once registered, log in to begin the application.
